chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI H.C.A. No.167 of 2011 M/s Qamran Construction (Pvt.) Ltd. Versus Saleemullah & others Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For hearing of main case. 2. For hearing of CMA 2223/11 Dated: 12.03.2024 Mr. Shehanshah Hussain for appellant. None for respondents. Mr. Dilawar Hussain Advocate on Court notice. -.-.- This appeal is pending since 2011 which impugns order dated 26.09.2011 in terms whereof Mr. Dilawar Hussain was shown to be co- liquidator and at his request in that capacity office was directed to place on record cases filed by or against Qamran Construction (Pvt.) Ltd. Mr. Dilawar Hussain who was present on the said date and stated that he was appointed as co-liquidator of Shaikh Sajjad & Sons and gave a very bold statement that a number of properties of Qamran Construction Private Limited are under liquidation. Today he has filed a statement and states that he found that it was a wrong statement given; he must have realized this much earlier and should have come forward on his own and not on account of Court notices which have been issued to him on numerous occasions on which he has not appeared and/or not responded. Be that as it may, Mr. Shehanshah Hussain submits that if such statement is taken on record and the impugned order is modified excluding Qamran Construction Private Limited from being considered as under liquidation, he would not object to such delayed statement of Mr. Dilawar Hussain to be taken on record and disposal of the appeal in terms thereof. The statement is accordingly taken on record. The impugned order, to the extent where it demonstrate liquidation of Qamran Construction Private Limited and/or its properties, is recalled and set aside.

The statement is accordingly taken on record. The impugned order, to the extent where it demonstrate liquidation of Qamran Construction Private Limited and/or its properties, is recalled and set aside. Impugned order be read only to the extent of Sheikh Sajjad & Sons only. Appeal stands allowed to the above extent and disposed of accordingly along with listed application. Judge Judge
